questions.q: In the Ramayana, what does the word 'dharma' broadly refer to?
questions.explanation: Dharma is the principle of righteousness, moral law, and duty that every figure in the epic is expected to uphold.

questions.q: Rama is celebrated above all in the Ramayana for his unfailing adherence to which principle?
questions.explanation: Rama's steadfast commitment to dharma is described as the very spine of the story.

questions.q: Out of duty to honor his father's word, what did Rama agree to do?
options: Go into forest exile for fourteen years
questions.explanation: Upholding his father Dasharatha's promise to Kaikeyi, Rama dutifully accepted fourteen years of forest exile.

questions.q: Whose promise to Queen Kaikeyi did Rama feel duty-bound to fulfill?
questions.explanation: King Dasharatha had granted Kaikeyi two boons, and Rama upheld dharma by honoring his father's word.

questions.q: Rama is often given which title for being the ideal upholder of dharma?
questions.explanation: Rama is honored as Maryada Purushottam, the perfect man who never transgresses the bounds of righteousness.

questions.q: According to the epic, dharma must be upheld even against which inner forces?
questions.explanation: The card describes dharma as the moral law upheld against desire and grief.

questions.q: Which brother followed Rama into exile out of devotion and a sense of duty?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana chose to accompany Rama into the forest, embodying loyal duty toward his elder brother.

questions.q: What is the title of the card whose subject is dharma?
questions.explanation: The dharma card is titled 'Dharma' with the subtitle 'The Path of Righteousness'.

questions.q: Rama's wife Sita demonstrated dharma by choosing to do what during his exile?
questions.explanation: As a devoted wife, Sita insisted on following Rama into the forest rather than staying behind in comfort.

questions.q: Who is the revered sage credited as the author of the Valmiki Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The Ramayana that establishes Rama as the embodiment of dharma is attributed to the sage Valmiki.

questions.q: When offered the throne after Rama's exile, how did Bharata uphold dharma?
options: He went to war against Rama
He banished Kaikeyi from the kingdom
He refused to rule himself and placed Rama's sandals on the throne
questions.explanation: Bharata refused the crown won by his mother's scheming and ruled in Rama's name, enshrining Rama's paduka (sandals).

questions.q: Why did Rama ultimately help Sugriva against Vali, and how is this debated in terms of dharma?
options: He allied with Sugriva and slew Vali, a deed later questioned on grounds of fair combat
He refused to interfere in the monkeys' dispute
He crowned Vali king of Kishkindha
He fought Vali in open single combat
questions.explanation: Rama killed Vali from concealment to aid the exiled Sugriva, an act Vali himself challenged as a breach of warrior dharma.

questions.q: Which rakshasa abandoned his brother Ravana on grounds of dharma and joined Rama?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana left Ravana after counseling him to return Sita, choosing righteousness over kinship, and allied with Rama.

questions.q: What act of Ravana is presented as the central violation of dharma in the epic?
questions.explanation: Ravana's forcible abduction of Sita is the great adharma that sets Rama's righteous war in motion.

questions.q: Which aged vulture king died upholding dharma while trying to rescue Sita from Ravana?
questions.explanation: Jatayu fought Ravana to stop Sita's abduction and was mortally wounded, dying in Rama's arms as a model of duty.

questions.q: Lakshmana's wife Urmila is praised for which act of quiet dharma during the exile years?
options: Remaining in Ayodhya in patient devotion while Lakshmana served Rama
Following the brothers into the forest
questions.explanation: Urmila stayed behind in Ayodhya, bearing the long separation so Lakshmana could fulfill his duty to Rama.

questions.q: How did King Dasharatha's own conduct reflect the demands of dharma after granting Kaikeyi's boons?
options: He kept his given word though it broke his heart and led to his death from grief
He abdicated and joined Rama in exile
questions.explanation: Bound by his promise, Dasharatha honored his word and exiled Rama, then died of grief, embodying truth at terrible cost.

questions.q: What does the term 'raja-dharma' specifically refer to in the Ramayana?
options: The duty of a wife to her husband
The penance of a forest hermit
The righteous duties of a king toward his subjects
The code of a merchant's trade
questions.explanation: Raja-dharma is the duty of a king to rule justly and protect his people, a standard Rama exemplifies as the ideal ruler.

questions.q: Which devoted servant of Rama is held up as a model of selfless duty (seva) in the epic?
questions.explanation: Hanuman's tireless, humble service to Rama makes him the supreme example of dharma expressed through devotion.

questions.q: Before the war, what did Rama and Vibhishana urge to give Ravana one last chance at dharma?
options: That Ravana peacefully return Sita to avoid destruction
That Ravana surrender his crown to Kumbhakarna
questions.explanation: The righteous path offered was for Ravana to restore Sita, but his refusal sealed his fate.

questions.q: Which concept card closely related to dharma names Rama as 'The Perfect Man'?
questions.explanation: The 'Maryada Purushottam' card celebrates Rama as the perfect man, the human ideal of dharma.

questions.q: How is the great war against Ravana characterized in the moral framework of the Ramayana?
options: As a dispute over land and gold
As a contest for a throne
As a war of dharma against adharma
As a personal feud between brothers
questions.explanation: The conflict is framed as righteousness (dharma) confronting and overcoming unrighteousness (adharma).

questions.q: Vali, when struck down, accused Rama of breaching which specific aspect of warrior dharma?
options: Using a divine weapon against a mortal
Striking him from hiding rather than in open, face-to-face battle
questions.explanation: Vali charged that Rama, hidden, shot him while he fought Sugriva, violating the fairness expected between warriors.

questions.q: By tradition, who narrate the Ramayana, the song of Rama's dharma, in the court of Rama himself?
options: Lava and Kusha, Rama's twin sons
questions.explanation: Valmiki taught the epic to Sita's sons Lava and Kusha, who sang it before Rama at the Ashvamedha sacrifice.

questions.q: In the Uttara Kanda, Rama's painful decision to send the pregnant Sita to the forest is justified by which dharma?
options: Raja-dharma, a king's duty to honor public opinion above personal happiness
questions.explanation: Citing his duty as king to maintain the people's trust, Rama placed raja-dharma above his own love for Sita.

questions.q: Which sage's hermitage did Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana visit on the advice that it marked a righteous abode early in the exile?
options: Sage Agastya's in the deep south
Sage Bharadwaja's, which pointed them toward Chitrakoot
questions.explanation: Bharadwaja received the exiles near Prayaga and directed them to settle at Chitrakoot, a place fit for dharmic living.

questions.q: The doctrine that one should follow one's own duty imperfectly rather than another's well is best captured by which term echoed in dharmic thought?
questions.explanation: Sva-dharma, doing one's own appointed duty, underlies why each character in the epic acts according to their station.

questions.q: When Bharata sought to bring Rama back, on what dharmic ground did Rama refuse to return early?
options: Honoring his father's command and vow took precedence even over Bharata's plea
He no longer wished to be king
He had taken a vow of permanent renunciation
questions.explanation: Rama held that fulfilling Dasharatha's word for the full term was the higher dharma, and so declined the throne for then.

questions.q: Which boatman, often named Guha, is remembered for his humble act of dharma in ferrying Rama across the Ganga during the exile?
questions.explanation: Guha, the Nishada (fisherfolk) chief, devotedly aided Rama at the Ganga, honored for serving dharma despite low birth.

questions.q: The famous line 'dharmo rakshati rakshitah' expresses which idea central to the epic?
options: Dharma rewards those who renounce it
Dharma protects those who protect dharma
questions.explanation: The maxim teaches that those who uphold righteousness are in turn protected by it, the moral promise underlying Rama's path.
